concerns.text: Task T020 iterates over 'species (He/p, Fe/p)' but omits the mandatory control analysis for absolute, rigidity-normalized fluxes required by FR-008 and US-2. The control analysis is a distinct requirement to establish baseline modulation magnitude, not just a subset of ratio calculations. T021 exists but is scoped as a separate step rather than part of the per-rigidity-bin iteration logic mandated by FR-008.

Task T030 calculates R² but omits the task to perform the significance test (e.g., F-test or p-value for R²) required by SC-004 to confirm the model explains a 'statistically significant portion' of the variance. Without this, the success criterion cannot be fully verified.

Task T007 creates base data entities but fails to explicitly mandate the 'rigidity' attribute in the CosmicRayFlux entity. This violates Constitution Principle VI (Rigidity-Dependent Flux Calibration) and FR-001, which require rigidity-binned data for all flux measurements. Without this field, subsequent normalization and per-bin analysis are impossible.

Task T016 requires validating 'at least one full solar cycle (2011-2024)' with no critical missing blocks. This conflicts with the Spec's Assumptions (Data Availability) which mandate a graceful degradation fallback to the 'most populated rigidity bin' if data is missing. T016's hard validation risks pipeline failure instead of the required adaptive behavior.

Task T014 description lists 'He/p, Fe/p' but the implementation instruction is ambiguous regarding the specific heavy species logic (CNO vs Fe) compared to the strict FR-003 requirement to exclude zero denominators for *both* ratios. While the text mentions Fe/p, the lack of explicit handling for the 'CNO/Fe' distinction in the task description creates a risk of silent implementation errors.
